name: Sunroot Commons
desc: A community-focused eatery serving seasonal dishes harvested from nearby vertical gardens. Guests dine beneath living canopies powered by solar skylights.

name: Verdant Lotus
desc: Fresh pho, bánh mì, and herb-forward specialties made with locally grown produce. Floating garden terraces surround the dining area.

name: Solar Spice Collective
desc: A vibrant restaurant offering regional Indian curries, dosas, and street food. Its rooftop greenhouse supplies many of the spices and vegetables used daily.

name: Pollinator's Table
desc: Known for wood-fired flatbreads, olive dishes, and fresh salads. Native pollinator gardens bloom throughout the property.

name: Aurora Bamboo Kitchen
desc: Serving sushi, ramen, and seasonal small plates in a bamboo-framed structure. Soft bioluminescent lighting creates a peaceful evening atmosphere.

name: Rainwater Cantina
desc: A lively cantina featuring tacos, mole dishes, and fresh aguas frescas. The building showcases innovative rainwater harvesting systems.

name: Skyvine Noodle House
desc: Hand-pulled noodles and regional specialties served beneath suspended vine gardens. Solar-powered kitchens operate throughout the day.

name: The Circular Harvest
desc: A zero-waste restaurant transforming local harvests into creative plant-based meals. Compost generated on-site supports neighboring community gardens.

name: Windflower Mezze
desc: Offering hummus, shawarma, and freshly baked pita in a breezy courtyard setting. Decorative wind turbines help power the restaurant.

name: Emerald Tide Bistro
desc: Specializing in sustainably sourced seafood paired with locally cultivated greens. Panoramic windows overlook New Verdania's algae-lined waterways.

name: Golden Mycelium Café
desc: A cozy café known for mushroom-inspired dishes, artisan breads, and seasonal soups. Many furnishings are grown from sustainable mycelium composites.

name: Helios Hearth
desc: Serving bibimbap, barbecue, and comfort food prepared using solar thermal cooking systems. Murals celebrate the city's renewable future.

name: Moonpetal Patisserie
desc: An elegant bakery crafting pastries, fruit tarts, and herbal teas using ingredients grown in nearby rooftop orchards. Gentle moonlight panels illuminate the evening café.

name: Canopy Ember Grill
desc: A warm gathering space serving flame-grilled vegetables, skewers, and hearty grain bowls. Living trees grow through the open-air dining hall.

name: Blue Fern Taverna
desc: Inspired by coastal Greece, this tavern serves mezze platters, seafood dishes, and herb-infused drinks. Vertical fern walls naturally cool the interior.

name: Copper Kelp Kitchen
desc: A creative dining destination featuring sustainable sea vegetables, noodles, and fusion specialties. Decorative aquaponic channels weave through the restaurant.

name: Sunblossom Arepa House
desc: A cheerful eatery serving arepas, roasted vegetables, and tropical juices sourced from local greenhouse cooperatives.

name: The Moss Lantern
desc: Guests enjoy handcrafted dumplings and tea beneath glowing moss lanterns cultivated by local bio-designers. Rain gardens surround the tranquil courtyard.

name: Riverleaf Smokehouse
desc: Known for slow-smoked plant proteins, seasonal vegetables, and rustic sides. Waste heat from neighboring greenhouses helps power the kitchen.

name: Starlight Paella Garden
desc: Serving Spanish paellas and tapas in a garden illuminated by soft solar lanterns. Fragrant citrus trees line the pathways between tables.

name: Bloomforge Brewery & Kitchen
desc: A community brewpub producing small-batch botanical ales alongside hearty seasonal dishes. Reclaimed materials and living walls define the industrial-chic atmosphere.

name: Crescent Orchard Brasserie
desc: A refined brasserie offering seasonal tasting menus inspired by New Verdania's fruit orchards and community farms.

name: Thistle & Sun Café
desc: A welcoming neighborhood café serving artisanal sandwiches, seasonal salads, and floral-infused beverages. Sun-tracking glass panels maximize natural light throughout the day.

name: Verdelight Tagine House
desc: Specializing in Moroccan tagines, couscous, and mint teas prepared with produce from nearby community gardens. Intricate solar lanterns cast warm patterns across the dining hall.
